subject: Mathematics
title: Ad Hominem: Attacking the Person
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Ad Hominem: Attacking the Person.

hook.text: 'You're wrong because you're a terrible person.' That's not an argument—it's an attack.

buildup.text: An ad hominem fallacy dismisses an argument by attacking the character of the person making it.

discovery.text: A claim is true or false regardless of who says it. Evaluating the messenger is not evaluating the message.

twist.text: Sometimes character matters: a known liar's testimony is less reliable. But the logic still stands separately.

climax.text: Political debates overflow with ad hominem attacks because they're emotionally effective—even though they're logically empty.

punchline.text: Attack the argument, not the person. That's logic.

quiz.question: What is an ad hominem fallacy?
options: Attacking the person instead of their argument
